_readme: Copy this file to config/local-config.json (gitignored) and fill in your values.
azure.clientSecret and the cribl.auth credentials are read ONLY by the local host from that file; they are never exposed over HTTP.

cribl.leaderUrl: the leader base URL WITHOUT the /api/v1 suffix (the host appends it). Cribl.Cloud workspace: https://main-{orgId}.cribl.cloud (workspace name 'main' unless yours is renamed); on-prem leader: https://host:9000.
cribl.auth picks how the host authenticates to the leader. The 'cloud' example below is the primary path; the other two shapes are in _criblAuthVariants - copy the one you need over cribl.auth.
auth type 'cloud': OAuth client credentials created in the Cribl.Cloud UI under Organization > API Credentials. The host mints bearer tokens at login.cribl.cloud and refreshes them before expiry.

auth type 'onprem': self-managed leader username/password. The host logs in via {leaderUrl}/api/v1/auth/login and re-logs-in automatically when the leader returns 401. Not available against Cribl.Cloud.
auth type 'token': a static bearer token you minted yourself. The host cannot refresh it; when it expires, leader calls surface 401s until you update the config.
Back-compat: the legacy flat cribl.authToken string still works (treated as auth type 'token') but is deprecated and logs a startup note.
cribl.rejectUnauthorized: set false ONLY for self-signed on-prem leader certificates; applies to leader calls only (the Cribl.Cloud token mint always verifies TLS).
port: the loopback port the host listens on (http://127.0.0.1:{port}).
